bills.title: Universal Basic Compute Right Act

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Digital Rights
Committee Report Filed - Favorable with Amendments

bills.fullText:
AN ACT to establish a Universal Basic Compute Right guaranteeing all verified citizens of the Principality of Aetheria access to a baseline allocation of compute resources on the public network for personal projects, learning, and civic participation.

SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE.
This Act may be cited as the "Universal Basic Compute Right Act of 2026".

SECTION 2. FINDINGS.
The Assembly finds that:
(1) Compute resources have become the fundamental substrate of digital existence;
(2) Equitable access to compute is essential for meaningful participation in the digital polity;
(3) The Principality's public network possesses sufficient capacity to guarantee a baseline allocation to all verified citizens without compromising network integrity;
(4) A Universal Basic Compute Right aligns with Article 3 of the Digital Sovereignty Charter.

SECTION 3. UNIVERSAL BASIC COMPUTE RIGHT.
(a) ENTITLEMENT. Every verified citizen shall be entitled to a monthly allocation of compute credits equivalent to 500 compute-hours on the public network's standard tier.
(b) PORTABILITY. Compute credits shall be portable across compatible workloads and non-transferable between citizens.
(c) ROLLOVER. Unused credits may roll over for up to three (3) months, capped at 1500 compute-hours.

SECTION 4. ADMINISTRATION.
The Ministry of Infrastructure shall administer the allocation system, ensuring:
(1) Transparent, auditable allocation via smart contract;
(2) Anti-abuse measures including rate limiting and fraud detection;
(3) Quarterly public reports on utilization and network impact.

SECTION 5. FUNDING.
The Treasury shall allocate 5% of quarterly network fees to the Universal Compute Fund.

SECTION 6. EFFECTIVE DATE.
This Act takes effect 90 days after enactment.

amendments.title: Amendment: Expand Allocation to 750 Compute-Hours
amendments.text: Amend Section 3(a) to increase the monthly allocation from 500 to 750 compute-hours.

amendments.title: Amendment: Add Roll-over Cap Increase
amendments.text: Amend Section 3(c) to increase the rollover cap from 1500 to 2000 compute-hours.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-203@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Esteemed colleagues, this Act establishes a fundamental right that recognizes compute as the oxygen of digital life. Every citizen deserves the means to breathe.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-089@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: I support the principle but propose Amendment 001 to increase the allocation. 500 hours is insufficient for meaningful AI/ML workloads.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-156@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: The rollover cap increase in Amendment 002 is a reasonable compromise. Adopted by voice vote.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-312@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Concerned about network congestion. The Ministry of Infrastructure must guarantee QoS for priority workloads.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-445@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: The Treasury allocation of 5% is sustainable per the Q2 Infrastructure Report. This is fiscally responsible.

bills.title: Open-Source Infrastructure Mandate

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Infrastructure & Technology

bills.fullText:
AN ACT to mandate that all infrastructure contracts funded by the Treasury release their core protocol code under an OSI-approved license within 180 days of deployment.

SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE.
This Act may be cited as the "Open-Source Infrastructure Mandate Act".

SECTION 2. MANDATE.
(a) Any infrastructure project receiving Treasury funding exceeding 10,000 AETH tokens shall publish its core protocol source code under an OSI-approved license within 180 days of mainnet deployment.
(b) "Core protocol" includes consensus logic, networking layer, state transition functions, and cryptographic primitives.
(c) Exemptions may be granted by the Ministry of Security for components directly related to cryptographic key management or validator identity.

SECTION 3. COMPLIANCE.
The Ministry of Infrastructure shall maintain a public registry of funded projects and their compliance status.

SECTION 4. ENFORCEMENT.
Non-compliant projects shall be ineligible for future Treasury funding for a period of 24 months.

SECTION 5. EFFECTIVE DATE.
This Act takes effect immediately upon enactment.

bills.title: Regional Ambassador Program Act

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Outreach & Expansion

bills.fullText:
AN ACT to establish a funded Regional Ambassador Program to support citizen onboarding, local node operation, and community building in underrepresented regions.

SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE.
This Act may be cited as the "Regional Ambassador Program Act".

SECTION 2. PROGRAM ESTABLISHMENT.
The Ministry of Outreach shall establish a Regional Ambassador Program with the following objectives:
(1) Accelerate citizen onboarding in Africa, South America, and Southeast Asia;
(2) Support local node operators through technical guidance and stipends;
(3) Foster regional communities aligned with the Charter and Manifesto.

SECTION 3. AMBASSADOR ROLES.
Ambassadors shall:
(1) Conduct monthly onboarding workshops;
(2) Operate or support at least one public node in their region;
(3) Submit quarterly reports to the Ministry of Outreach.

SECTION 4. FUNDING.
The Treasury shall allocate 50,000 AETH tokens annually to the Ambassador Fund.

SECTION 5. OVERSIGHT.
The Assembly Committee on Outreach shall conduct semi-annual reviews.

SECTION 6. EFFECTIVE DATE.
This Act takes effect immediately.

amendments.title: Amendment: Add Oceania Region
amendments.text: Amend Section 2(1) to include Oceania as a target region.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-156@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: This program operationalizes our commitment to global inclusion. The Charter demands we reach beyond traditional tech hubs.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-777@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Oceania has a vibrant developer community that has been underserved. Amendment 003 ensures they're included from day one.

bills.title: Digital Sovereignty Charter Amendment: Cross-Network Citizenship Recognition
bills.fullText:
PROPOSED AMENDMENT to Article 4 of the Digital Sovereignty Charter.

CURRENT TEXT:
"Article 4: Citizenship. Citizenship in the Principality of Aetheria is granted to natural persons who complete the verification process and affirm the Charter and Manifesto."

PROPOSED TEXT:
"Article 4: Citizenship. Citizenship in the Principality of Aetheria is granted to natural persons who complete the verification process and affirm the Charter and Manifesto. The Principality recognizes valid citizenship credentials issued by compatible digital polities adhering to the Inter-Polity Recognition Protocol (IPRP), subject to verification and reciprocity agreements ratified by the Assembly."

EXPLANATORY NOTE:
This amendment enables cross-network citizenship recognition, allowing citizens of compatible digital nations to participate in Aetheria's governance without duplicative verification, provided their polity meets IPRP standards and reciprocity is established.

debateLog.speaker: President Hermes
debateLog.text: This amendment is the next step in our evolution from a single polity to a network of networks. Interoperability is sovereignty.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-312@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Concerned about dilution of citizenship standards. The IPRP must be rigorous and auditable.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-203@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: This enables the vision of a federation of digital nations. The Charter envisioned this from Article 1.

bills.title: Treasury Transparency & Audit Act

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Treasury & Oversight

bills.fullText:
AN ACT to mandate quarterly public audits of the Treasury, real-time on-chain dashboard of all inflows/outflows, and independent audit firm rotation every 2 years.

SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE.
This Act may be cited as the "Treasury Transparency & Audit Act".

SECTION 2. QUARTERLY AUDITS.
An independent auditor shall conduct a full audit of Treasury holdings, transactions, and smart contract interactions quarterly.

SECTION 3. REAL-TIME DASHBOARD.
The Ministry of Infrastructure shall deploy and maintain a public, real-time dashboard of all Treasury on-chain activity.

SECTION 4. AUDITOR ROTATION.
No audit firm shall serve more than two consecutive 2-year terms.

SECTION 5. EFFECTIVE DATE.
This Act takes effect 60 days after enactment.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-445@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Transparency is not optional. The Treasury manages public funds; the public deserves real-time visibility.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-888@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: The Committee found this duplicative. The Treasury already publishes quarterly reports and the dashboard is in development (infra-q2-2026). Mandating auditor rotation disrupts institutional knowledge.
